No Poverty.reason: Career development equips individuals with skills and opportunities for employment, directly supporting access to economic resources (Target 1.4) and thus indirectly contributing to poverty reduction.
evidence: effective management of your own career

Zero Hunger.reason: There is no explicit or inferable connection between career development and ending hunger or ensuring food security in the course syllabus.

Good Health and Well-being.reason: The course explicitly covers "work-family" issues (Week 13), which directly relates to promoting mental health and well-being, a key aspect of Target 3.4.

Quality Education.reason: As a university course, it provides quality tertiary education (Target 4.3) and explicitly aims to develop relevant skills for employment and career management (Target 4.4).
evidence: develop skills to analyze and manage complex problems

Gender Equality.reason: The course's focus on "work-family" issues and effective career management directly relates to addressing gender-specific career barriers and promoting equal opportunities for women (Target 5.5).
evidence: effective management of your own career

Clean Water and Sanitation.reason: There is no explicit or inferable connection between career development and clean water or sanitation in the course syllabus.

Affordable and Clean Energy.reason: There is no explicit or inferable connection between career development and affordable, clean energy in the course syllabus.

Decent Work and Economic Growth.reason: The course's entire focus is on "Career Development," directly addressing the achievement of full, productive, and decent employment for all (Target 8.5) and reducing youth unemployment (Target 8.6).

Industry, Innovation and Infrastructure.reason: Preparing students for the "modern workplace" and "global careers" indirectly contributes to developing human capital for industrial sectors and fostering innovation (Targets 9.2, 9.5).

Reduced Inequalities.reason: Empowering individuals through career development can lead to better economic opportunities, promoting economic inclusion (Target 10.2) and reducing inequalities of outcome (Target 10.3).

Responsible Consumption and Production.reason: The core competency to "identify sustainability issues" can influence students' career choices towards companies adopting sustainable practices, indirectly supporting Target 12.6.
evidence: Students will understand and be able to identify ethics, social responsibility, and sustainability issues.

Climate Action.reason: While "sustainability issues" are mentioned, there is no specific or strong inferable connection to climate action within the course syllabus.
